title: Off-Market Victorian Terrace in Chiswick
excerpt: How the Johnson family secured a 4-bedroom Victorian terrace in Chiswick before it ever reached the open market.
content:
The Johnsons had been searching for 14 months. Every suitable home in Chiswick went within days of listing, always above asking. They came to us frustrated and ready to give up on their target area.

We activated our local network and within 10 days had an off-market introduction: a 4-bedroom Victorian terrace whose owner was retiring and wanted a discreet, chain-free sale. No brochure, no viewings, no Rightmove listing.

We arranged an exclusive viewing, ran full due diligence — planning history, structural assessment, flood risk — and negotiated a price £24,000 below the owner's initial expectation. The family exchanged in 9 weeks.

"We'd been trying alone for over a year. Advaith solved it in 10 days. The off-market access is genuinely extraordinary."

title: Remote Purchase from Singapore
excerpt: How Dr. Sarah Chen bought a 5-bedroom family home in Surrey without setting foot in the UK until completion day.
content:
Dr. Chen was relocating from Singapore with her family. She had a 6-week window to secure a family home before the school term started. She had never bought in the UK before and couldn't visit in person during the search phase.

We became her eyes and ears on the ground. Over three weeks we shortlisted 12 properties, visited and video-reviewed 8, ruled out 5 on structural or planning grounds, and presented 3 serious options with full written reports.

We negotiated her chosen property to £30,000 below asking using comparable sales data and a 28-day completion incentive. Our in-house legal coordination managed the entire conveyancing remotely.

Dr. Chen landed at Heathrow on completion day and collected her keys within 3 hours. The children started school the following Monday.

title: First-Time Buyers in Bristol — Getting It Right
excerpt: Alex and Jamie thought they were priced out of the Bristol market. Three weeks later they owned a 2-bedroom flat off-market.
content:
Alex and Jamie Roberts had a £320,000 budget and were told repeatedly by estate agents they'd need to compromise heavily in Bristol's competitive market. Two offers had already fallen through — both times outbid by cash buyers.

We began by assessing the strategy. Rather than competing on Rightmove, we targeted a specific postcode in Clifton where we knew two landlords were considering selling. Within a week we had a direct approach agreed — a 2-bedroom flat that had never been listed publicly.

The agreed price came in at £298,000 — £22,000 under budget. We used the saving to fund a full Level 3 survey, which identified £4,000 of minor works we negotiated off the final price.

Completion was in 8 weeks, entirely chain-free. Alex and Jamie are now mortgage-free in their first home with money to spare.

title: Three-Property Portfolio in 18 Months
excerpt: How the Patel family built a three-property BTL portfolio producing 7.8% average gross yield, guided by Advaith from start to finish.
content:
The Patels came to us with £250,000 to deploy across multiple BTL acquisitions. Their previous attempts at self-directed BTL had resulted in one overpriced property in an area with structural oversupply of rentals.

We began with a portfolio strategy session — identifying target yields (7%+ gross), suitable areas (East Midlands and Yorkshire), and property types (3-bedroom semi-detached with strong tenant demand).

Over 18 months we sourced, negotiated, and completed three acquisitions: a semi in Nottingham at 7.6% gross, a terrace in Sheffield at 8.1% gross, and a mid-terrace in Derby at 7.7% gross. All three were purchased below comparable market value.

The portfolio now generates £3,200/month gross rental income from a £680,000 total investment. We continue to act as the family's property partner for future acquisitions.

title: Luxury Apartment in Canary Wharf — Below Market
excerpt: How Michael Thompson secured a premium apartment in Canary Wharf at a significant discount through off-market developer negotiations.
content:
Michael had been watching a specific Canary Wharf development for 18 months, waiting for the right unit. When a 2-bedroom river-view apartment came available, the developer's asking price was £650,000 — at the top of comparables for the floor.

We were able to open a direct conversation with the developer's sales director through our professional network. By presenting a credible, chain-free cash buyer with a 28-day target, we secured a 7.5% discount: a purchase price of £601,250.

The saving of £48,750 more than covered our fee many times over. We also negotiated a covered parking space (worth £25,000) included in the purchase price.

Michael completed on schedule. The apartment is now yielding 4.8% gross as a furnished short-let, with an estimated capital uplift of 12% in the 18 months since purchase.

title: Saving a Complex Chain in Hertfordshire
excerpt: Mark and Angela Foster nearly lost their dream family home when a complex 4-chain transaction threatened to collapse. Here's how Advaith held it together.
content:
The Fosters had agreed on a detached 5-bedroom home in Harpenden — perfect school catchments, large garden, accessible commute. The problem: a 4-chain transaction with a first-time buyer at the bottom who lost their mortgage offer 6 weeks from exchange.

At the point of crisis we stepped in as chain coordinator. We identified a replacement buyer, introduced them to a broker who secured an AIP within 24 hours, and negotiated a 3-week extension from the seller at the top of the chain.

We also identified and resolved a leasehold complication that could have derailed the title transfer — a historical covenant that required a specialist indemnity insurance policy and liaison with the original estate's solicitors.

The chain exchanged 5 weeks later. The Fosters completed on schedule. Without active chain management, this purchase would certainly have collapsed.
